index_contract.identity_rule: Observation-target scope is a provenance-bearing edge and never implies physical-object identity or canonical containment.

cache_contract.key: verified content SHA-256 when supplied; otherwise source/release/product locator fingerprint plus retrieval version
cache_contract.promotion: download to a bounded temporary file, verify size/type/checksum, then atomically promote
cache_contract.eviction: least-recently-used among unpinned reproducible payloads; preserve published, cited, active-job, unique-source, and operator-pinned products
cache_contract.durability: The USB bulk cache is replaceable and may not be the only copy of an irreplaceable source artifact.
cache_contract.failure_mode: A missing or corrupt payload remains visibly unavailable; never substitute a different product silently.

security_contract.locator_policy: Only registry-approved source hosts and protocols may be fetched; user-supplied arbitrary URLs are forbidden.
security_contract.redirect_policy: Redirect targets must pass the same source allowlist and protocol checks.
security_contract.size_policy: Require a per-request byte ceiling before transfer and abort when the observed response exceeds it.
security_contract.content_policy: Treat products as untrusted data, never executable content; validate media type and parse in bounded workers.
security_contract.path_policy: Cache paths are derived from encoded identifiers or hashes and cannot contain caller-controlled traversal segments.

payload_triggers: bounded prefetch from measured public interest
forbidden: payload blobs in CORE or ARM hot databases
